skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields,...
skill.summary: Create gallery-quality algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness, interactive parameter controls, and reproducible generative algorithms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. Network URLs are legitimate CDN resources for p5.js and Google Fonts. The flagged code patterns (regex.exec, seeded randomness, file reading) are all legitimate for generating algorithmic art. No confirmed security risks identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Network URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate CDN resources: p5.js library from cdnjs.cloudflare.com and Google Fonts. Expected for a web-based generative art skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate CDN URLs for p5.js and Google Fonts - required for the skill to function

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell/Backtick Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged backticks as shell execution. These are markdown code fences in SKILL.md for syntax highlighting, not actual shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown code block delimiters in documentation, not shell execution operators

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Python exec() Function
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 'exec()' at generator_template.js:133. This is JavaScript regex.exec() method, not Python exec() or process execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Code at line 133 is 'regex.exec(hex)' - a standard JavaScript regex method for parsing hex color codes

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Database
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 'SAM' at viewer.html:508. This is part of 'PARTICLE SYSTEM' comment, not Windows Security Account Manager.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line contains comment '// PARTICLE SYSTEM' - scanner misidentified substring 'SYSTEM' containing 'SAM'

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged various lines as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithms present - scanner produced false positives on license text and documentation

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude and Claude Code to generate gallery-quality algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness for reproducible results and interactive parameter exploration.
actual_capabilities: Create algorithmic philosophy documents that guide generative art creation
Build p5.js sketches with seeded randomness for reproducible outputs
Design interactive HTML artifacts with parameter controls for user exploration
Generate flow fields, particle systems, and emergent visual patterns
Export self-contained HTML files that work in Claude artifacts and browsers
Implement Art Blocks-style seeded art with prev/next/random seed navigation
limitations: Output is limited to p5.js-based generative art in HTML format
Does not generate static image files directly (only HTML artifacts)
Requires internet access to load p5.js from CDN
Performance depends on browser capabilities for complex generative algorithms

use_cases.title: Digital Artists Seeking Unique Visuals
use_cases.description: Create one-of-a-kind generative art pieces with mathematical beauty and emergent behavior. Perfect for digital artists wanting algorithmic creativity.

use_cases.title: Educational Tool for Teaching Generative Art
use_cases.description: Use as a teaching tool to demonstrate concepts like seeded randomness, flow fields, particle systems, and parametric design.

use_cases.title: Rapid Prototyping for Art Installations
use_cases.description: Quickly generate and explore algorithmic art variations to find inspiration for larger art projects or installations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Flow Field Art
prompt_templates.prompt: Create algorithmic art using flow fields. Use Perlin noise to drive particle movement. Include parameters for particle count, noise scale, and trail length. Make the colors respond to velocity.

prompt_templates.title: Design Geometric Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create geometric algorithmic art inspired by sacred geometry. Use recursive subdivision or L-systems. Include parameters for recursion depth, angle, and scale. Emphasize mathematical precision.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Emergent Crystallization
prompt_templates.prompt: Create art based on stochastic crystallization. Use circle packing or Voronoi tessellation with relaxation. Parameters should include density, cell size, and color palette. Each seed should produce unique crystalline beauty.

prompt_templates.title: Build Interactive Art Explorer
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sophisticated algorithmic art piece with multiple tunable parameters. Include seed navigation (prev/next/random) and export capability. The algorithm should feel meticulously crafted with expert-level parameter tuning.

output_examples.input: Create algorithmic art with flow fields and particle trails
output_examples.output: An interactive HTML artifact with a p5.js canvas showing flowing particle trails driven by Perlin noise. Sidebar controls include seed input, previous/next/random buttons, and sliders for particle count (100-5000), noise scale (0.001-0.01), flow speed (0.5-5), and trail length (0-255 alpha). Colors shift from cool to warm based on particle velocity.

output_examples.input: Generate recursive geometric art with golden ratio
output_examples.output: Self-contained HTML showing branching recursive structures. Each branch subdivides based on golden ratio constraints. Parameters include max depth (3-10), branch angle (15-45 degrees), length decay (0.5-0.8), and color scheme selection. Seed navigation allows exploring 100 variations.

best_practices: Always use seeded randomness (randomSeed, noiseSeed) for reproducible outputs
Start from the provided viewer.html template to maintain Anthropic branding
Design parameters that feel natural to the algorithm, not arbitrary controls
anti_patterns: Do not create HTML from scratch without using the provided template structure
Avoid hardcoded colors - use parameter-driven palettes for exploration
Do not skip seed navigation controls - reproducibility is core to the value

faq.question: What is algorithmic art?
faq.answer: Algorithmic art is visual art created using computational algorithms. Instead of drawing manually, you write code that generates visuals through mathematical rules, randomness, and emergent behavior.

faq.question: How does seeded randomness work?
faq.answer: Seeded randomness uses a starting number (seed) to initialize random number generation. The same seed always produces the same sequence of random values, making output reproducible. This is similar to Art Blocks style generative art.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools. The generated HTML artifacts work as Claude artifacts and can be opened in any browser.

faq.question: What is a flow field?
faq.answer: A flow field is a visualization technique where a grid of vectors (often from Perlin noise) guides particles across the canvas. Particles leave trails showing the underlying field structure, creating organic, flowing visual patterns.

faq.question: How do I explore different variations?
faq.answer: The generated artifacts include seed navigation controls: Previous, Next, Random buttons, and a seed input field. You can explore 100 preset seeds or enter any positive integer for unique variations.

faq.question: Can I export the generated art?
faq.answer: Yes. The artifacts include a Download PNG button that saves the current canvas as an image file. This works in both Claude artifacts and regular browsers.
